title: geigeria hoffmanniana subsp. obovata
intro: Geigeria hoffmanniana subsp. obovata, also known as Geigeria obovata, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. Traditionally, it has been used in South African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including the ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat digestive problems. Geigeria obovata can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, and its roots and leaves can be used separately or in combination to harness its medicinal properties.
